_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised)
_meta.note: VA Fredericksburg-area state delegation (state tier). Tara Durant (SD-27, R) + Joshua Cole (HD-65, D). Evidence-based finalization from primary roll-call records (VPAP/LIS) + issue scorecards (ReproRising, Giffords, ChoiceTracker). Durant: genuinely conservative record with a VERIFIED same-sex-marriage YES vote (HJ3, 01/16/2026) as the key deviation. Cole: uniformly progressive, chief sponsor of the abortion-rights AND same-sex-marriage amendments.

set.office: State Senator, District 27 (sitting · 2026 candidate for Congress, VA-7)

profile.religion: Self-describes as faith-grounded; teaches at Holy Cross Academy (Catholic school), Stafford; denomination not publicly confirmed
profile.background: Virginia State Senator, District 27 (Fredericksburg/Stafford/Spotsylvania), sitting since Jan 2024; former Delegate HD-28 (2022-2024). Educator/librarian; 'Marine wife,' three children. Now running for U.S. House VA-7 (R primary Aug 2026).

tara-durant.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-27 (sitting since Jan 2024); former Delegate HD-28. Running for U.S. House VA-7 in 2026 (one-record dual-office: current office is the sitting Senate seat). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Genuinely conservative HARD record: consistent pro-life voting (graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising; voted NO on the SJ247 reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment); sponsored parental-notification gender bills (HB1707, 'Sage's Law') and a parents'-rights bill on sexually-explicit school material; voted against SB1031 (homeschool restriction); sponsored a vehicle-tax-relief credit + ALEC member; tough-on-crime (fentanyl penalties, Marcus alert). KEY DEVIATION (verified via VPAP primary roll call): voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, 01/16/2026; and the 2025 first reference) — biblical_marriage q0/q1 FALSE. Election-integrity and 2A specifics rest on caucus-unity inference (92%), not isolated roll calls, so left null pending a cited vote.

0.note: Consistent pro-life voting record; graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising; voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247, 2025).
Voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, 01/16/2026; and 2025 first reference) — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted YES to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Sponsored HB1707 (parental notification when a student presents as the opposite sex) and 'Sage's Law'; biological-sex framing on school policy.

4.note: Sponsored parental-notification gender bills opposing promotion of gender ideology in state-funded schools.

0.note: Voted against SB1031 (2025), which she argued would restrict homeschooling freedom; defends homeschool freedom.

1.note: Sponsored 'Sage's Law' + HB1711 (parental access to a minor's medical records) — parental notification/consent on minors' gender/medical decisions.

2.note: Introduced a parents'-rights bill requiring notification + alternative assignments for sexually-explicit instructional material.
Sponsored SB1443 (vehicle personal-property tax-relief credit); backed no-tax-increase bipartisan budgets; ALEC member.

0.note: Tough-on-crime: sponsored SB655 (fentanyl) and SB602 (Marcus alert); voted against criminal-justice-leniency expansions.

2.note: Sponsored enhanced fentanyl-possession penalties — proportional punishment of evildoers.

profile.religion: Christian; clergy — assistant pastor, Union Bell Baptist Church (BA, Liberty University)
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, District 65 (Fredericksburg area); served 2020-2022 and since 2024; re-elected Nov 2025. Baptist pastor; first Black person elected from the Fredericksburg region to the General Assembly. Publicly identifies as bisexual.

joshua-cole.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-65 (Fredericksburg area); Baptist pastor; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record and an active LEADER of the measures most opposed by this rubric: CHIEF SPONSOR of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJR1; A+/'Champion' from ReproRising) and CHIEF SPONSOR of the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3; he is himself bisexual). Led automatic voter registration + felon-voting-rights restoration. Gun-control endorsed (Giffords: voted for safe-storage + universal background checks). Supported pro-transgender school policy; opposes book 'bans.' No conservative deviations found. Confirms the prior 0/70 read. (Note: this record was de-duplicated 2026-06 — a second 'joshua-g-cole' HD-65 record was merged in.)

0.note: Chief sponsor of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; A+/'Champion' from ReproRising — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted the full pro-abortion-access slate; opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Chief sponsor of the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban; openly bisexual.

1.note: Chief sponsor / YES on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

4.note: Supported promotion of LGBTQ-affirming policy in schools.

0.note: Opposes school choice/ESAs (Democratic-caucus alignment); centers public-school funding.

1.note: Supported transgender-student-friendly school policy rather than parental-notification requirements.

2.note: Opposes library book 'bans'/removals; supports a 'more diversified curriculum.'
Voted to raise the minimum wage and expand education/health spending (Democratic budget priorities).

1.note: Led automatic voter registration and felon-voting-rights restoration — opposite of photo-ID/citizenship-verification restrictions.

2.note: Expanded voting access rather than tightening early/absentee voting.

1.note: Giffords-endorsed; voted for safe-storage laws and mandatory background checks.

3.note: Voted FOR universal background checks (opposite of the 2A position).
